message:
refactor(sync,mcp): distinguish lock contention from lock-path errors

Five-file refactor propagating a new error-boundary distinction: "the
sync lock is held" vs "the sync lock path is broken". Previously both
collapsed to None and silently skipped the auto-import / auto-flush
path, which masked real filesystem misconfigurations.

Core change (src/sync/mod.rs):
  try_sync_lock: Option<File>  →  Result<Option<File>>
    - Ok(Some(lock)): acquired
    - Ok(None):       another process holds it (WouldBlock)
    - Err(_):         open() failed or OS lock call returned Error
  Added a unit test that wedges the lock path (by creating a dir where
  the .sync.lock file should live) and asserts the Config error
  message surfaces the path.

Downstream fan-out:
  - src/cli/commands/sync.rs: detect_prefix_from_jsonl now returns
    Result<Option<String>> instead of silently skipping malformed
    JSONL. Switched to read_issues_from_jsonl() which already handles
    parse errors. New test covers a malformed line before the first
    valid issue.
  - src/main.rs: auto-import path handles the three-way match on
    try_sync_lock and logs at debug when skipping.
  - src/mcp/mod.rs: new helpers auto_flush_mcp_error,
    sync_lock_mcp_error, sync_lock_busy_error,
    dirty_auto_flush_incomplete_error. These return structured
    McpError::with_data payloads so MCP clients can branch on
    error_type (AUTO_FLUSH_FAILED, SYNC_LOCK_UNAVAILABLE) instead of
    string-matching.
  - tests/e2e_sync_fuzz_edge_cases.rs: +58 lines covering the new
    error paths end-to-end.

No user-facing behaviour change on the happy path; broken-lock-path
and dirty-flush failures now surface cleanly instead of silently
degrading.
